name: tomato vinaigrette
instructions:
MACERATE: combine finely diced shallots and crushed garlic with red wine vinegar and balsamic vinegar. Let sit for 15 minutes. This softens the raw allium edge and lets the vinegars take on their flavor.

GRATE THE TOMATO: peel one tomato, then grate it on a cheese grater into the bowl. The grater pulls the tomato into a loose pulp that integrates with the vinegars rather than sitting as chunks.

FINISH: stir in olive oil, salt, and freshly cracked black pepper to taste. The black pepper matters here — coarse, freshly cracked black pepper carries the dressing in a way pre-ground pepper doesn't.
notes:
From Samin Nosrat (Salt Fat Acid Heat).

BLACK PEPPER QUALITY MATTERS: this dressing is built on a small set of clean components, and one of them is black pepper. Use freshly cracked, coarsely ground pepper — pre-ground pepper has lost most of its volatile oils and the dressing will read flatter as a result.

WHY MACERATE: the 15-minute soak of the alliums in the vinegars does two jobs: it mellows the harshness of the raw shallot and garlic, and it pre-loads the vinegars with allium flavor. Skipping the maceration leaves you with a dressing that reads raw and harsh.

TOMATO PEELING: peel the tomato before grating. The skins curl into hard strips on the grater and end up as unpleasant bits in the finished dressing.
